key_insight: GAIA learns geometry-aware operator mappings by encoding boundary and interior information into tokens, enabling one model to handle both forward and inverse PDE problems on arbitrary domains.
engineering_relevance: This can accelerate simulation, reconstruction, and design workflows by replacing iterative solvers with a fast amortized surrogate that works across changing geometries.
method: Encodes domain boundary and interior field samples into geometry tokens.
Uses cross-attention to condition integral transform layers on local geometric features.
Supports forward, boundary value, and inverse problems without retraining, graph construction, or iterative optimization.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on seven 2D/3D benchmarks, including four new or extended inverse/BVP tasks, where GAIA achieved new state of the art and reduced median relative L2 error by 64% on airfoil flow reconstruction and 27% on EIT versus the next best amortized method.
watch_item: Performance may depend on accurate geometry encoding and sufficient training coverage of shape variability, so unseen domain classes or very sparse observations could still challenge generalization.
paper_title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
